name: Alligator and Andouille Sauce Piquante
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub both sides of alligator meat with Cajun seasoning and cut into 1 inch by 1 inch pieces. If possible, allow to marinate overnight.
2. Brown alligator in olive oil over high heat. Remove from pot.
3. Saute andouille in the same oil for 5 minutes and remove from pot.
4. Pour tomato sauce into the pot with the remaining oil. Stir sauce over high heat until it is very brown, burned.
5. Keep stirring until a thick paste forms. Add margarine, roux, chicken base, onions, bell pepper, celery, cayenne pepper, jalapeno peppers, and sugar. Saute until onions are clear.
6. Return alligator and andouille to the pot. Add garlic, mushrooms, and 2 quarts of water. Bring to a boil and then reduce to medium heat.
7. Cook for 1 hour, adding water as needed. Once alligator is tender, add green onions and parsley.
8. Cornstarch mixture may be added to thicken gravy. Serve over hot cooked rice.
9. Chicken, squirrel, rabbit, turtle, or duck may be substituted for the alligator. Regular smoked sausage may be substituted for the andouille.
